candidates.name: Laura Kelly
candidates.notes: Two-term Dem governor in deep-red KS. Catholic. NRA 'F' rating — endorsed by Bloomberg's Everytown for Gun Safety. Vetoed 24+ bills in 2026 session alone plus 31 budget line items. Vetoed HB 2311 (religious beliefs/child welfare placement re: sexual orientation/gender identity). Vetoed born-alive bill. Vetoed NRA-backed K-12 gun safety training program. Vetoed school voucher/private school funding expansion. Vetoed bills restricting voter registration and mail-in balloting. Vetoed anti-DEI college bill. Vetoed bill lowering concealed carry age to 18. However: signed 2 pro-gun bills (Apr 2025) — seized firearms resale to FFLs and provisional license upgrades. Beat NRA-backed Kris Kobach in 2018. KS legislature overrides multiple vetoes on free speech, life, education (per ADF).

profile.education: Bradley University (BS, Psychology); Indiana University (MS, Recreation Administration)
profile.background: Grew up in a military family; Kansas state senator (2005-2019); became 48th governor in 2019.

claims.text: Vetoed HB 2311 (religious beliefs/child welfare placement re: sexual orientation/gender identity).

candidates.name: Jerry Moran
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from KS since 2011 (previously US Rep KS-1, 1997-2011). Heritage Action LIFETIME 64% — session scores: 74% (112th), 63% (114th), 77% (117th). Moderate-conservative establishment Republican. Christian Methodist. NRA 'A' rating. NRA donated $23,850+ to his campaigns since 1998. Chair of Senate Veterans' Affairs Committee and Appropriations subcommittee. Supports strong defense spending and veteran services. Kansas farm/rural background. More bipartisan than movement conservatives — worked across aisle on veterans issues, broadband, mental health. Not a firebrand — quiet, effective legislator.

profile.education: University of Kansas (BA Economics), University of Kansas School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Kansas state senator and U.S. House member. Attorney in private practice. Former university instructor. In Senate since 2011. Appropriations Committee member.
profile.office: 521 Dirksen Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Roger Marshall
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from KS since 2021 (previously US Rep KS-1, 2017-2021). Heritage Action LIFETIME 82% — strong upward trend: 53% (115th House), 93% (116th House), 94% (117th Senate), 89% (118th Senate). Physician (OB/GYN — delivered 5,000+ babies). Nondenominational Christian — attends First Christian Church, Great Bend, KS where he served as elder, deacon, and board chair. Taught Sunday School with wife for 25+ years. NRA A+ rating. Strong pro-life (OB/GYN background gives medical credibility). Kansas farm country roots. Served on Senate Agriculture, Health, and Appropriations committees. M.D. credential gives him unique platform on healthcare debates.

profile.education: Kansas State University (BS Biochemistry), University of Kansas School of Medicine (MD)
profile.background: Obstetrician who delivered over 5,000 babies. Former U.S. House member. Army Reserve veteran. Practiced medicine in Great Bend, Kansas for 25 years.
profile.office: 479a Russell Senate Office Building
